text.title: persona-building Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for persona-building as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Creating personas with no interview data, using demographics or market reports as the source.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fabricated personas produce wrong product decisions. A persona without transcript citations is a hypothesis dressed as a finding.

failure-mode.description: Creating 4+ personas at seed stage and trying to design for all of them simultaneously.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multiple personas split focus and produce muddled messaging. One primary persona until the product has traction and ARR to justify more.

failure-mode.description: Including stock-photo demographics (age, city, salary) that no one on the team ever references in a product decision.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Demographic fields that never change a decision are dead weight. Include a demographic field only if it drives a specific product or messaging choice.

failure-mode.description: Publishing a persona document and never updating it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: By month 6 the actual ICP has often shifted. A stale persona still drives copy. Quarterly review or kill the artifact.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Who do we say no to?" shapes sales qualification and product scope more concretely than the primary persona. Define it early and link it to the sales-disqualification SOP.

failure-mode.description: Asking an LLM to "write a persona for indie SaaS founders" without supplying transcript data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs will produce plausible-sounding frustrations, quotes, and demographics from training data priors rather than your actual customers. Every persona trait must be grounded in a transcript citation.

failure-mode.description: Running synthesis at temperature above 0.3 for clustering and trait extraction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Higher temperature fabricates variation that looks like diverse customer segments. Use temperature 0 for clustering; reserve creative phrasing only for the optional characteristic quote field.

failure-mode.description: Feeding 30+ interview transcripts into one synthesis call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Long-context dumps cause the model to collapse to averages and lose outliers. Use map-reduce: cluster per transcript, merge cluster summaries, synthesise the merged summaries.

failure-mode.description: Including demographic fields (age, city, income) without 5+ transcript agreements.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs encode demographic stereotypes from training data. "32-year-old freelance designer in Brooklyn" will appear plausibly even when none of your interviews confirm it. Strip demographics from the synthesis prompt unless the data supports them.

failure-mode.description: Always feeding transcripts in the same order to the synthesis agent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs exhibit recency and primacy bias; the first and last transcripts in a batch disproportionately shape the output. Shuffle transcript order between runs; if the persona changes drastically, the data is too thin for a stable finding.
